- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).

- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## ASSISTANT (turn 9)

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

dispatch lifecycle

polling per REQ-58-07), not just WHAT.

```

## Key Decisions
- **D-58-21 LOCKED**: BATS 71-74 renumbering — slot 67 already occupied by G1 grep-guard `tests/scripts/tmux/67-delegate-task-no-native-task-tool.bats`.
- **D-58-36**: Verification gate for PLAN-08 = REAL UAT (human-driven, META-04), NOT BATS-only.
- **D-58-38**: RED-FIRST — all 4 BATS scenarios authored with `(red)` marker BEFORE any implementation.
- **D-58 (size)**: Max 500 LOC per module constraint enforced.
- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **Path decision**: 18 atomic commits remaining across 3 specialist domains (tmux, delegation, session-tracker/streaming) + 3 docs files + 2 templates = **coordinated-path via `hm-coordinator`** (multi-wave, multi-specialist, dependent). Fast-path reserved for I5 alone if dispatched solo.
- **Wave 4 split**: M1-M4 can run in parallel (4 doc/template commits), M5 last (REAL UAT evidence + 58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md).
- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor must use `git add <specific-files>` for atomic commits, never `git add .` or `git add -A`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## Goal
- Resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution — 18+ atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recovering progress from external platform agent whose work was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

- **D-58-21 LOCKED**: BATS 71-74 renumbering — slot 67 already occupied by G1 grep-guard `tests/scripts/tmux/67-delegate-task-no-native-task-tool.bats`.
- **D-58-36**: Verification gate for PLAN-08 = REAL UAT (human-driven, META-04), NOT BATS-only.
- **D-58-38**: RED-FIRST — all 4 BATS scenarios authored with `(red)` marker BEFORE any implementation.
- **D-58 (size)**: Max 500 LOC per module constraint enforced.
- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **Path decision**: 18 atomic commits remaining across 3 specialist domains (tmux, delegation, session-tracker/streaming) + 3 docs files + 2 templates = **coordinated-path via `hm-coordinator`** (multi-wave, multi-specialist, dependent). Fast-path reserved for I5 alone if dispatched solo.
- **Wave 4 split**: M1-M4 can run in parallel (4 doc/template commits), M5 last (REAL UAT evidence + 58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md).
- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor must use `git add <specific-files>` for atomic commits, never `git add .` or `git add -A`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor must use `git add <specific-files>` for atomic commits, never `git add .` or `git add -A`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

Task: Wire startPolling into src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts
dispatch() lifecycle. After the spawn that creates a delegated session, call

within 1s of child tool.execute.after (REQ-58-07 AC).

- npm run typecheck exit 0
- npm test exit 0 (or unchanged 3,310/3,310 with the new BATS added)

dispatch lifecycle

Commit body must explain WHY (post-spawn delegated session capture-pane
polling per REQ-58-07), not just WHAT.

- git add . or git add -A (working tree has 30+ auto-save files)
- Bundling with other tasks
- Adding "any" types
- Touching delegation-status.ts (already done in bd8b07e1)

startPolling is called, list of files touched (must be 1).
```

(a) Adjust the prompt first,

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## ASSISTANT (turn 70)

**Các lựa chọn khả thi (xin chỉ định 1):**

1. **Restart session với `task` tool available** — Tôi dừng tại đây; bạn restart OpenCode session với tool config enable `task` (native SDK subagent dispatch). Sau đó tôi dispatch S1-I5 ngay.

2. **Dùng `create-governance-session`** — Bypass `delegate-task` block, nhưng có side effects:
   - Auto-commit workspace state qua git (có thể commit cả 30+ `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-save files + `.planning/STATE.md` — vi phạm atomic commit discipline)
   - Title prefix `hm-governance:` (không match với S1-I5 execution semantics)
   - Không có cơ chế WaiterModel completion dual-signal
   
   Không khuyến nghị cho implementation task.

3. **Bạn tự chạy lệnh** — Tôi output exact bash commands cho bạn paste vào terminal; bạn commit S1-I5 manual rồi tôi continue orchestration với Waves 2B-2D-3-4.

4. **Gọi `execute-slash-command`** với command `gsd-execute-phase` — Dùng GSD command engine thay vì direct subagent dispatch. Có thể work nhưng mất control path decision + fast-path/coordinated-path distinction của mình.

5. **Skip S1-I5, chuyển sang Wave 2B (S2) hoặc Wave 3 (verification)** — Nếu S1-I5 work thực ra đã được external platform agent làm rồi (chỉ thiếu tracking), bạn xác nhận và tôi move on.

**Bối cảnh quan trọng:**
- Working tree dirty với 30+ auto-save files — bất kỳ commit nào từ session-tool side-effect đều có nguy cơ bundle các files này vào (vi phạm atomic commit rule + dirty-tree handling instruction trong landscape).
- 3 commits S1 (I1-I3) đã land `0cd8bff0 / 498992fc / bd8b07e1`. Có khả năng I4 cũng đã land (external platform) nhưng tôi chưa verify.
- 4 RED BATS tests 71-74 đã sẵn sàng — `4bfd1532 / 0f3b2b45 / 7d216809 / 8d06186a`.

Tôi sẽ **dừng tại đây** theo chỉ dẫn của bạn, chờ bạn chọn option 1-5 hoặc chỉ thị khác.

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

- **D-58 (size)**: Max 500 LOC per module constraint enforced.
- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **Path decision**: 18 atomic commits remaining across 3 specialist domains (tmux, delegation, session-tracker/streaming) + 3 docs files + 2 templates = **coordinated-path via `hm-coordinator`** (multi-wave, multi-specialist, dependent). Fast-path reserved for I5 alone if dispatched solo.
- **Wave 4 split**: M1-M4 can run in parallel (4 doc/template commits), M5 last (REAL UAT evidence + 58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md).
- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor must use `git add <specific-files>` for atomic commits, never `git add .` or `git add -A`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.
- **Reuse vs new landscape**: `.hivemind/planning/phase-57-58-loop-2026-06-03/landscape.md` (11646b) exists from earlier phase loop — evaluate whether to reference-and-extend or write fresh at `ses-p58-73-parent/landscape.md`.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## Goal
- Resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution — 18+ atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recovering progress from external platform agent whose work was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool (synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with parent-child hierarchy). `task` tool not in current L0 function list → `execute-slash-command` is the only available bypass for slash-command execution with `subtask: true`.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## Key Decisions
- **D-58-21 LOCKED**: BATS 71-74 renumbering — slot 67 already occupied by G1 grep-guard `tests/scripts/tmux/67-delegate-task-no-native-task-tool.bats`.
- **D-58-36**: Verification gate for PLAN-08 = REAL UAT (human-driven, META-04), NOT BATS-only.
- **D-58-38**: RED-FIRST — all 4 BATS scenarios authored with `(red)` marker BEFORE any implementation.
- **D-58 (size)**: Max 500 LOC per module constraint enforced.
- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **PATH-S1-I5 = fast-path**: Direct to `hm-l2-executor` (single specialist, known routing, immediate execution, depth=1). **PATH DECISION INTACT but execution path BLOCKED by harness delegate-task policy.**
- **PATH-W2B-2D = coordinated**: To `hm-coordinator` (implementation wave) — multi-specialist, dependent waves, depth grows to 2.
- **PATH-W3 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-validator` + `hm-l2-verifier` — 5 sequential verification gates, dual-signal completion per Q5.
- **PATH-W4-M1-M4 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-writer` (docs wave) — 4 docs-only commits can run parallel within docs wave.
- **PATH-META-04 = HUMAN**: Operator runs 4-step REAL UAT — D-58-36 LOCKED; FAIL = HARD FAIL (phase does not ship).
- **Wave 4 split**: M1-M4 can run in parallel (4 doc/template commits), M5 last (REAL UAT evidence + 58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md).
- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor uses `git add <specific-files>`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.
- **Landscape filename**: Used `landscape-2026-06-04.md` (date-stamped) instead of `landscape.md` per `.planning/AGENTS.md` rule for generated artifacts.
- **D-EXEC-01 (NEW)**: `execute-slash-command` with `subtask: true` and `parentSessionID: ses_170a8728dffeVpBkWU8vYgZ5RI` is the L0 fallback for `task` tool — works for `gsd-*` commands but NOT for arbitrary `hm-*` subagent dispatch (gsd-executor is the closest equivalent for development work).
- **D-EXEC-02 (NEW)**: gsd-executor reports `plan_count: 0, incomplete_count: 0` for phase 58 → SDK has no active plans registered. This is the AUTHORITATIVE state per the gsd index, OVERRIDES grep "I4/I5 not done" finding until user confirms work is actually in progress vs. already shipped.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **`task` tool is the correct delegation tool** (works in this session; returns `task_id` for stacking, e.g., `ses_17098b7cdffelNFjA48iYLtq4E`); `delegate-task` is **HARNESS-BLOCKED for code/artifact editing** — message: `delegate-task is a wrapper tool for async background delegation. Code and artifact editing work should use native task for synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with full parent-child hierarchy tracking. Use instead: task({ subagent_type: 'specialist-agent', prompt: '...' })`.
- `execute-slash-command` is available and works (used to invoke `gsd-execute-phase` which delegated to `gsd-executor` via `task` internally).
- Per dev policy: "ROUTE everything to gsd-* from commands, agents to workflows" for harness development work.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (but for harness dev work, `gsd-*` specialists are also valid per dev policy override).
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

- Wave 2B (S2) and Wave 2D (S4) both touch `manager-runtime.ts` and `coordinator.ts` — strong file-share dependency → sequential not parallel.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool (synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with parent-child hierarchy). `task` tool IS in L0 function list (used successfully — gsd-executor returned result via task id `ses_17098b7cdffelNFjA48iYLtq4E`).
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- `execute-slash-command` with `subtask: true` returned `[Tool execution was interrupted]` — UNRELIABLE; prefer native `task` tool.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.
- **L0 surface-level investigation permitted**: bash + read + grep for verification of state (Iron Law #11 — read/offset/skim).

- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.

- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.

- **Native `task` tool** for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task`); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn; ALWAYS prefer stacking onto existing sessions via `task_id` / `stackOnSessionId` before spawning new.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).

- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.

- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## Goal
- Resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution — 18+ atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recovering progress from external platform agent whose work was not recorded in our session-tracker. **User's original (auto-mode-repeated) message**: "I am in the middle of phase 58 gaps planning execution - the process is carrying by another agent in another platform hence the keeping track is not record correctly please track back and continue the work" — auto loop continues until gap plan is truly executed and verified.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test/auto-mode purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only", "auto mode" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool (synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with parent-child hierarchy). `task` tool IS in L0 function list (used successfully — gsd-executor returned result via task id `ses_17098b7cdffelNFjA48iYLtq4E`).
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- `execute-slash-command` with `subtask: true` returned `[Tool execution was interrupted]` — UNRELIABLE; prefer native `task` tool.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.
- **L0 surface-level investigation permitted**: bash + read + grep for verification of state (Iron Law #11 — read/offset/skim).
- **Auto mode rule (NEW)**: when user is in auto mode and message loops, CONTINUE WORK without stopping to ask. Only stop if genuinely blocked with no forward path.

## Goal
- Execute Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) end-to-end: 18+ atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recover progress from external platform agent whose session-tracker was not recorded; user explicitly directed: "this will continue until the gap plan created in phase 58 is truly executed and verified".

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool (synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with parent-child hierarchy). `task` tool IS in L0 function list but last attempt returned `[Tool result missing due to internal error]` — UNRELIABLE.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.
- **USER AUTO-MODE DIRECTIVE (NEW, 2026-06-04)**: "I am in the middle of phase 58 gaps planning execution - the process is carrying by another agent in another platform hence the keeping track is not record correctly please track back and continue the work". "This will continue until the gap plan created in phase 58 is truly executed and verified". **L0 MUST EXECUTE the gap plan autonomously — not just track/plan. User override of "ask for clarification" pause. The previous "operator decision required" is RESCINDED.**
- **L0 surface-level investigation permitted**: bash + read + grep for verification of state (Iron Law #11 — read/offset/skim).

## Key Decisions
- **D-58-21 LOCKED**: BATS 71-74 renumbering — slot 67 already occupied by G1 grep-guard `tests/scripts/tmux/67-delegate-task-no-native-task-tool.bats`.

- **D-58 (size)**: Max 500 LOC per module constraint enforced.
- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **PATH-S1-I5 = fast-path**: Direct to `hm-l2-executor` (single specialist, known routing, immediate execution, depth=1). **PATH DECISION INTACT — can execute via native `task` tool with `gsd-executor` subagent + custom prompt (since SDK sees 0 plans).**
- **PATH-W2B-2D = coordinated**: To `hm-coordinator` (implementation wave) — multi-specialist, dependent waves, depth grows to 2.
- **PATH-W3 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-validator` + `hm-l2-verifier` — 5 sequential verification gates, dual-signal completion per Q5.
- **PATH-W4-M1-M4 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-writer` (docs wave) — 4 docs-only commits can run parallel within docs wave.
- **PATH-META-04 = HUMAN**: Operator runs 4-step REAL UAT — D-58-36 LOCKED; FAIL = HARD FAIL (phase does not ship).
- **Wave 4 split**: M1-M4 can run in parallel (4 doc/template commits), M5 last (REAL UAT evidence + 58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md).
- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor uses `git add <specific-files>`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.
- **Landscape filename**: Used `landscape-2026-06-04.md` (date-stamped) instead of `landscape.md` per `.planning/AGENTS.md` rule for generated artifacts.

Second attempt returned `[Tool result missing due to internal error]`. This is the AUTHORITATIVE path for code/artifact execution when working; retry on failure.

- **D-EXEC-03 (LOCKED)**: gsd-executor reports `plan_count: 0, incomplete_count: 0` for phase 58 → SDK has no active plans registered. PLAN-08 exists in file system with valid frontmatter (`gap_closure: true` flag distinct from standard plans) but is NOT indexed. Standard `/gsd-execute-phase 58` is no-op.

- **D-DISPATCH-01 (NEW)**: Native `task` tool dispatch chain for code/artifact work in PLAN-08:

## Goal
- Resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution — 18+ atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recovering progress from external platform agent whose work was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool (synchronous, first-class subagent dispatch with parent-child hierarchy). `task` tool not in current L0 function list → `execute-slash-command` is the only available bypass for slash-command execution with `subtask: true`.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.
- **Continue-or-stop rule**: when user asks "continue or stop", do NOT stop to ask — only stop if genuinely blocked; otherwise advance the work.

### Blocked
- `execute-slash-command` with `subtask: true` returned `[Tool execution was interrupted]` once — UNRELIABLE for slash command execution (but `task` tool works, so not a hard blocker).
- External platform agent's task completions not recorded in our session-tracker — reconstructed from git log only.
- Local branch is **12 commits ahead of `origin/feature/harness-implementation`** (not pushed).
- `ses-child-73-21710` in error state — per R-58-R07: do NOT stack onto failed child; start fresh child via `task_id` (no stackable sessions found).
- Working tree dirty: 30+ `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` files (auto-saves) + `.planning/STATE.md` (6-line counter drift) + untracked `.planning/debug/p58-*` + untracked `58-META-ANALYSIS.md` + untracked `58-PLAN-08-GAP-FIX.md` + untracked `.hivemind/planning/ses-p58-73-parent/` — executor MUST use `git add <specific-files>`, never `git add .` or `git add -A`.
- **`create-governance-session` not safe for implementation tasks**: auto-commits workspace state via git (would bundle dirty files into commit) and uses `hm-governance:` title prefix (semantic mismatch with S1-I5 execution).
- **PLAN-08 not registered with SDK plan_index**: PLAN-08 file exists with valid frontmatter (incl. `gap_closure: true` flag), but `gsd-executor` reports `plan_count: 0` → SDK doesn't see PLAN-08 as a registered plan for Phase 58. Standard `/gsd-execute-phase 58` is no-op. Workaround: dispatch with custom prompt to gsd-executor.
- **Discrepancy between 58-CLOSE.md `SHIPPED` and live grep state**: 58-CLOSE says SHIPPED 2026-06-04T01:38:00Z, but manager-runtime.ts lacks I5 wire; PLAN-08 has `gap_closure: true` flag indicating post-ship gap-closure plan (not part of original close). Resolution: treat PLAN-08 as IN PROGRESS (gap_closure: true = explicitly post-ship work), proceed with I4/I5 then S2-S4 then META.
- **Trajectory slicing artifact**: source trajectory was sliced at the final user turn and did not include a tool result for a prior tool call. Underlying L0 execution is still continuing (Iron Law #12 check just ran) — slicing is a view-only artifact, not a state blocker.

## Key Decisions

- **D-58 (tdd)**: Hybrid mock pattern (capture + forward-to-real-tmux) — preferred for BATS tmux scenarios.
- **PATH-S1-I5 = fast-path**: Direct to `hm-l2-executor` (single specialist, known routing, immediate execution, depth=1). **PATH DECISION INTACT — can execute via native `task` tool with `gsd-executor` subagent + custom prompt (since SDK sees 0 plans).**
- **PATH-W2B-2D = coordinated**: To `hm-coordinator` (implementation wave) — multi-specialist, dependent waves, depth grows to 2.
- **PATH-W3 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-validator` + `hm-l2-verifier` — 5 sequential verification gates, dual-signal completion per Q5.
- **PATH-W4-M1-M4 = coordinated**: To `hm-l2-writer` (docs wave) — 4 docs-only commits can run parallel within docs wave.

- **Dirty tree handling**: `.hivemind/session-tracker/*.json` auto-saves must NOT be mixed with implementation commits — executor uses `git add <specific-files>`. May batch as single housekeeping commit at end.
- **Landscape filename**: Used `landscape-2026-06-04.md` (date-stamped) instead of `landscape.md` per `.planning/AGENTS.md` rule for generated artifacts.
- **D-EXEC-01 (LOCKED)**: `task` tool IS available in L0 function list and WORKS. ## Constraints & Preferences

- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3) — **RESCINDED for PLAN-08 implementation by D-EXEC-05 user auto-mode override + D-EXEC-06 infrastructure-failure override**.
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.

- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.

- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop.
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.

**ses_1708ad9c8ffeMk4wLWatkxI0Di:** ## STOPPING — Awaiting your decision on root-cause fix

I have a complete picture of the mismatch. I will not proceed without explicit confirmation because the literal plan instruction will not turn BATS 71 GREEN.

### The 30-second recap
- Plan says: wire `startPolling(delegationId, paneId)` into `dispatch()`
- Reality: `startPolling(intervalMs?)` is a global self-iterating loop; BATS 71 never calls `dispatch()`; `peek` only reads the cache and returns an error if it's empty

### Choose one (or pick "abort")

| Code | Fix | BATS 71 GREEN? | Touches |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Literal: add `startPolling()` call inside `dispatch()` | ❌ No | `manager-runtime.ts` only |
| **B** | `peek` falls back to fresh `capturePaneContent` when cache is empty | ✅ Yes | `delegation-status.ts` + deps type only |
| **C** | Do B now, defer A to a separate S1 plan | ✅ Yes | `delegation-status.ts` only this commit |

### My strong recommendation: **C** (or **B** if you want the smallest possible change)

B is the actual root cause — `peek` should not require the polling cache to be warm. BATS 71 is correctly testing that `peek` returns the live pane content after `send-keys`, which is a `capturePaneContent` concern, not a polling concern. A is a separate (legitimate) improvement — auto-start polling at dispatch time — but it doesn't make this test pass.

If you want me to proceed with A anyway (accepting BATS 71 stays RED), say exactly: **"proceed with option A"**.
If you want the root-cause fix, say: **"proceed with option B"** (or **"proceed with option C"**).

I am holding execution and will not write code, run tests, or commit anything until you reply with one of the above.

**Continuity index:** See `session-continuity.json` for active delegations and pending work at time of compaction.

## ASSISTANT (turn 10)

## Goal
- Execute Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) end-to-end: 29 atomic commits + 5 verification gates + 4 META docs + 1 human-driven REAL UAT (META-04 BLOCKING per D-58-36) — recover progress from external platform agent whose session-tracker was not recorded; user explicitly directed: "this will continue until the gap plan created in phase 58 is truly executed and verified".

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces; planning artifacts L5 only (not runtime readiness).
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; **date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`**; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- L0 WRITE allowed paths: `.md`/`.xml`/`.json` in `.hivemind/planning/**` only; DENIED writes to `src/`, `tests/`, `.opencode/agents/`.
- L0 READ restrictions: `(\.)?(docs?|plans?|plannings?)/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` + `.hivemind/**/*.{md,json,xml,yaml,txt}` only.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3) — **RESCINDED for PLAN-08 implementation by D-EXEC-05 user auto-mode override**.
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #7: NEVER repeat prompt when resuming — context is preserved in `task_id`.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions can be ignored if contradicted.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- **Harness policy: route everything to gsd-* from commands/agents/workflows for development work** (`/gsd-execute-phase` is the correct execution surface, not `delegate-task` for code/artifact editing).
- **Harness block**: `delegate-task` is BLOCKED for code/artifact editing — must use `task` tool.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop.
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists (or `gsd-*` for tooling).
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

**ses_1708ad9c8ffeMk4wLWatkxI0Di:** ## STOPPING — Awaiting your decision on root-cause fix

I have a complete picture of the mismatch. I will not proceed without explicit confirmation because the literal plan instruction will not turn BATS 71 GREEN.

### The 30-second recap
- Plan says: wire `startPolling(delegationId, paneId)` into `dispatch()`
- Reality: `startPolling(intervalMs?)` is a global self-iterating loop; BATS 71 never calls `dispatch()`; `peek` only reads the cache and returns an error if it's empty

### Choose one (or pick "abort")

| Code | Fix | BATS 71 GREEN? | Touches |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Literal: add `startPolling()` call inside `dispatch()` | ❌ No | `manager-runtime.ts` only |
| **B** | `peek` falls back to fresh `capturePaneContent` when cache is empty | ✅ Yes | `delegation-status.ts` + deps type only |
| **C** | Do B now, defer A to a separate S1 plan | ✅ Yes | `delegation-status.ts` only this commit |

### My strong recommendation: **C** (or **B** if you want the smallest possible change)

B is the actual root cause — `peek` should not require the polling cache to be warm. BATS 71 is correctly testing that `peek` returns the live pane content after `send-keys`, which is a `capturePaneContent` concern, not a polling concern. A is a separate (legitimate) improvement — auto-start polling at dispatch time — but it doesn't make this test pass.

If you want me to proceed with A anyway (accepting BATS 71 stays RED), say exactly: **"proceed with option A"**.
If you want the root-cause fix, say: **"proceed with option B"** (or **"proceed with option C"**).

I am holding execution and will not write code, run tests, or commit anything until you reply with one of the above.

**Continuity index:** See `session-continuity.json` for active delegations and pending work at time of compaction.

## ASSISTANT (turn 106)

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`.
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT task_id to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Stack with `task_id` when stackable session exists; do NOT repeat prompt on resume.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.

## ASSISTANT (turn 110)

## Goal
- Track back and resume Phase 58 gap-fix plan (PLAN-08) execution that was carried by an external platform agent whose progress was not recorded in our session-tracker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Governed by `.hivemind/AGENTS.md`: L0 does NOT mutate `.hivemind/**` runtime state directly; only typed modules own runtime-contract surfaces.
- Governed by `.planning/AGENTS.md`: Planning artifacts authorize future work but cannot claim runtime readiness; date-stamp generated artifacts `name-YYYY-MM-DD`; never blindly copy OMO/archived patterns; treat archived artifacts as historical reference only (recheck date).
- Governed by `.opencode/AGENTS.md` (delegation/skill rules, line ranges, scope enforcers).
- Iron Law #1: L0 NEVER executes inline (delegate to L2/L3).
- Iron Law #2: Form complete end-to-end landscape BEFORE delegating any piece.
- Iron Law #6: NEVER start new session when aborted exists — use EXACT `task_id` to resume.
- Iron Law #8: L0→L2/L3 fast-path allowed for single-specialist / known-routing tasks.
- Iron Law #9: NEVER skip quality gate triad (lifecycle → spec → evidence).
- Iron Law #10: MAX 3 skills loaded concurrently.
- Iron Law #11: NEVER read full files when grep/offset/skim works — line-aware reading.
- Iron Law #12: ALWAYS use session-tracker to find aborted sessions before starting fresh.
- **USER OVERRIDE rule**: For UAT/live-test purposes always prioritize THE USER's prompting — constitutions below can be ignored if contradicted; especially for "for testing only", "for uat only" framings.
- **Investigator rule**: If user prompt >10 lines OR response is absurd/short → MUST spawn sub-agents to investigate context and make clear before executing.
- Native `task` tool for dispatch (NOT `delegate-task` — on maintenance); yield control — no parallel `task` calls in one turn.
- Find-stackable-first via `delegation-status({action:"find-stackable"})` before dispatching.
- Checkpoints enforced via 11-checkpoint phase loop (Scouting → CRUD → Trajectory/Contract Init → Specification → Context → Research/Patterns → Planning → Execution → Verification → Ship).
- Generic agents PROHIBITED — always named `hm-*` specialists.
- Tooling consistency: execute checkpoints via `execute-slash-command` rather than inline task execution.
- Comm format: `phase-58-gap-fix(S{N}): <msg>`; RED tests committed with `(red)` marker before impl.
- Invariants: 27-tool-key preserved; P20 no-new-deps; AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression; max 500 LOC per module.
- Hybrid mock pattern: capture + forward-to-real-tmux.
- BATS grep-guard filters comment lines.
